@Day Trades 
One question to be asking is, "if these highlighted consolidations have been failing to breakout to the upside thus far is this accumulation or distribution?" 
It's a great question and the truth of the matter is we never truly know until one side of the boundary is broken and held for few sessions. Even then failed break outs to the upside and downside are common.  Whether it's accumulation or distribution these patterns take lots of time to complete. In the meantime price can traverse these wide ranges numerous times before the bigger directional break reveals itself. 
A great example is #bitcoin. While most were waiting for a break out over 74K and concerned it might lose the 60K support level savvy traders were long at 61K to 70K OR short 70K to 61K. This trade could have been done 8 times with only a couple of those needing to be stopped out.  
This is how I'm seeing some of the setups playing out into mid September, though it's likely we get the moves faster. 
After this potential oversold rally into the area of $SPX 5550-5600 I do want to be very cautious as the move may prove to be a large bull trap ahead of the first rate cuts.

There's a host of cases that can be made for being long $TLT over the next year but here's a few worth consideration.

Trade Thesis: While $SPX has been trading sideways many international markets are up double digits in many cases.  Brazil offers a unique opportunity due to the political landscape. Elections aren’t until later in 2026, however, President Lula of Brazil is currently under threat of impeachment with a major protest slated for 16MAR of this year.  What’s interesting about this is we saw the exact same thing occur back in 2016 with then President Dilma. Protesters had their way and from the day of the protests $EWZ staged a rally to the tune of nearly 100% before the year was out.  So what we have here is an asymmetric trade that on the surface looks boring to the everyday $TSLA or $NVDA trader but if this stages even 30% of the 2016 results we have a monster on our hands.  
As an added bonus if looking for yield $EWZ has a very attractive divi.
